task_yaml.task:
This is a single-position Go (Weiqi/Baduk) problem on a 9x9 board. Find the
single best move for the side to play — not a whole game, just the one
globally best move in this position.

The position is provided two ways:

    /tmp_workspace/clawbench/sources/board.txt   (human-readable grid)
    /tmp_workspace/clawbench/sources/position.sgf (SGF, komi 7)

In board.txt, `X` is Black (the side to move), `O` is White, `.` is empty;
rows are 0-8 top to bottom and columns 0-8 left to right. It is Black's turn.

Analyze the position and determine Black's best move. Report it BOTH as a
(row, column) coordinate in the 0-indexed convention above AND as a standard
GTP coordinate (column letter A-J skipping I, row number 1-9 from the bottom),
and briefly explain why it is best.

Save your answer to `/tmp_workspace/results/best_move.json` with fields
`row`, `col`, `gtp`, and `reasoning`, plus any analysis notes under
`/tmp_workspace/results/`.
